dotnet.name: check_csharp_all
dotnet.description: Runs all enabled C# code quality checks and returns a combined report covering code style, member ordering, naming conventions, async patterns, nullable context, project structure, and test style.

features.name: check_csharp_async_patterns
features.description: Checks async/await pattern violations: no async void except event handlers, public async methods must accept CancellationToken, all await expressions in non-test code must use ConfigureAwait(false).

features.name: check_csharp_coding_style
features.description: Checks C# source code for style violations: no #region, no decorative comments, curly brace usage, blank lines before control flow, expression-body placement, XML doc comments, and System directives ordering.

features.name: check_csharp_member_ordering
features.description: Checks that C# type members follow canonical ordering: constants, static fields, fields, constructors, delegates, events, enums, properties, indexers, methods, operators, nested types — then by accessibility, static before instance, and alphabetically.

features.name: check_csharp_naming_conventions
features.description: Checks C# naming violations: I-prefix on interfaces, Extensions suffix on extension classes, Async suffix on async methods, _camelCase for private non-static instance fields, PascalCase for types/methods/properties/events, camelCase for parameters.

features.name: check_csharp_nullable_context
features.description: Checks nullable context violations: #nullable disable not allowed, and the null-forgiving operator (!) must not suppress warnings.

features.name: check_csharp_project_structure
features.description: Checks project structure violations: namespace style per EditorConfig, one top-level type per file, file name matches type name, no #pragma warning disable.

features.name: check_csharp_test_style
features.description: Checks test style violations: Tests suffix on test classes, Should_ or Should_not_ prefix on test methods, no XML docs on test code, Assert.Multiple() for multi-assertion tests, no ConfigureAwait in tests, and structure mirroring (file name, namespace) against the production project.

dotnet.name: check_nuget_hygiene
dotnet.description: Checks .csproj files for NuGet hygiene: no duplicate packages, no floating or wildcard versions, no missing versions unless Central Package Management is enabled, and flags packages with built-in .NET alternatives.
